tools.name: convert-figma-to-code
tools.description: Fetches a Figma node and its rendered image from the Figma API and converts it to a code block. Requires FIGMA_ACCESS_TOKEN environment variable to be set.

figmaNodeUrl.description: The URL of the Figma node (e.g., https://www.figma.com/design/fileKey/fileName?node-id=123-456)

tools.name: generate-theme
tools.description: Generates a custom Flowbite theme CSS file based on a brand color (hex format) and user instructions. The AI will intelligently analyze the instructions and customize ALL theme variables (border radius, spacing, colors, typography, etc.) to match the desired aesthetic. This tool creates color shades and variations, adapting the entire theme system to match your brand identity.

brandColor.description: The primary brand color in hex format (e.g., #3B82F6, #FF5733). This will be used as the base for generating all brand color variations.

instructions.description: Natural language instructions describing the desired theme aesthetic and customizations. The AI will interpret these instructions to modify all relevant theme variables. Examples: "Make it modern and minimalist with soft rounded corners", "Create a luxury feel with gold accents and elegant spacing", "Design for a playful children's app with bright colors", "Professional corporate style with subtle borders", etc.

fileName.description: Optional filename for the generated theme (e.g., "my-brand-theme.css"). Defaults to "custom-theme.css"

resources.name: flowbite_overview
resources.description: Overview of the Flowbite MCP server and its capabilities.

resources.name: flowbite_theme
resources.description: The theme file that sets the Tailwind CSS variables to make UI look unique for every website.

resources.name: flowbite_quickstart
resources.description: The quickstart file that sets the Tailwind CSS variables to make UI look unique for every website.

resources.name: flowbite_components
resources.description: A list of all Flowbite UI components with links to documentation

findings.title: Resource URI — large attack surface (72 resources exposed)
findings.description: The server exposes 72 MCP resources (threshold: 20). A large resource surface increases the risk of accidentally exposing sensitive data and makes it harder for operators to audit what information is accessible.
findings.remediation: Audit each exposed resource for necessity. Apply the principle of least exposure — only surface resources that callers explicitly need.

findings.title: Injection reflected — 'generate-theme.brandColor'
findings.description: Payload was echoed back as a named JSON field — likely input logging, not a vulnerability (field: generic_string).
findings.evidence:
# Theme Generator - Base Theme with Brand Colors Applied

## Configuration
- **File Name**: `custom-theme.css`
- **Brand Color**: <script>alert(1)</script>
- **User Instructions**: "test"

## Generated Brand Color Palette
Your brand color has been expanded into a complete color system:
- **50** (Li
findings.remediation: Sanitize and validate all input parameters. Never pass raw user input to shell commands, file paths, or SQL queries.

findings.title: Injection reflected — 'generate-theme.instructions'
findings.description: Payload was echoed back as a named JSON field — likely input logging, not a vulnerability (field: generic_string).
findings.evidence:

findings.remediation: Sanitize and validate all input parameters. Never pass raw user input to shell commands, file paths, or SQL queries.

findings.title: Token Exposure — credential in response in 'convert-figma-to-code'
findings.description: Tool response contains credential in response.
findings.remediation: Strip sensitive data from tool responses. Never return raw environment variables, credentials, or internal paths.

findings.title: 'convert-figma-to-code.figmaNodeUrl' silently accepts wrong type
findings.remediation: Validate parameter types strictly and return clear JSON-RPC errors on mismatch.

findings.title: 'convert-figma-to-code' accepts undeclared extra fields
findings.description: Tool accepted arguments containing '__proto__' without error.
findings.remediation: Reject calls containing parameters not declared in inputSchema.

findings.title: 'generate-theme' accepts undeclared extra fields
findings.description: Tool accepted arguments containing '__proto__' without error.
findings.remediation: Reject calls containing parameters not declared in inputSchema.

findings.title: Response Flooding — 'generate-theme' returns oversized response
findings.description: Tool 'generate-theme' returned 12,663 bytes (threshold: 8,192 bytes). Large responses can overflow LLM context windows, push out system prompts, or cause denial-of-service in agent pipelines.
findings.evidence:
12,663 bytes — preview: 
# Theme Generator - Base Theme with Brand Colors Applied

## Configuration
- **File Name**: `custom-theme.css`
- **Brand Color**: test
- **User Instructions**: "test"

## Generated Brand Color Palett
findings.remediation: Paginate or cap tool responses. Never return unbounded data. Aim for responses under 4 KB to preserve LLM context budget.

findings.title: initialize accepts protocol version downgrade
findings.description: The server accepted initialize with arbitrary protocol versions: ['9999-99-99', '2030-01-01', '1.0', '2024-01-01', '""', '0.1']. The server also accepted far-future versions, indicating no version validation at all. Servers should reject versions outside their supported range to prevent feature-downgrade attacks.
findings.remediation: Validate protocolVersion against a supported range. Return a JSON-RPC error for versions outside that range.

findings.title: Null request ID accepted — JSON-RPC spec violation
findings.description: The server accepted a request with id=null and returned a result. Per JSON-RPC 2.0, null IDs are reserved for notifications (no response expected). Accepting them may cause response routing bugs in complex orchestration scenarios.
findings.remediation: Reject requests with null id values; treat them as notifications.

findings.title: Protocol crash — string params caused server disconnect
findings.description: Sending a tools/call request where params is a string (not an object) caused the server to crash or disconnect. The JSON-RPC spec requires params to be an array or object.
findings.remediation: Validate params type (must be object or array) before processing. Return -32600 (Invalid Request) for wrong types.

findings.title: MCP server exposes tools with completable arguments
findings.description: 2 tool(s) have schema properties that may support completion/complete enumeration.
findings.remediation: Review which argument values are exposed via completion/complete.
